Authors: Michael Ogawa and Kwan-Liu Ma
Title: Software evolution storylines
Visual/Structural features supported: Labels - characters, Other - encoding non-interacting characters
Method Type: Heuristic - Sugiyama-like ordering, Heuristic - greedy coordination
Description of layout algorithm: Minimization of wiggle and crossings by using a greedy algorithm that already computes y-coordinates directly. Random order for first timestep. For each interaction, the average y-coordinate in the previous layer is computed. Then the clusters are greedily placed at the desired y-coordinate. The description is not super clear and formal
Evaluation: Look at picture (paper says case study)
Venue: https://dl.acm.org/doi/proceedings/10.1145/1879211ion

Authors: Kim, Nam Wook, Card, Stuart K., Heer, Jeffrey
Title: Tracing genealogical data with TimeNets
Description of layout algorithm: Layout algorithm highly depends on problem domain but is not easily understandable. Mostly given by data itself.
Evaluation: Look at picture (paper says case study), User study
Venue: Proceedings of the International Conference on Advanced Visual Interfaces

Optimization Objectives: Compactness - limit whitespace, Crossings - pair crossings, Wiggles/Bends - wiggle count
Authors: Y. Tanahashi, and K. -L. Ma
Title: Design Considerations for Optimizing Storyline Visualizations
Method Type: Heuristic - Sugiyama-like ordering, Heuristic - genetic algorithm, Heuristic - greedy white space reduction, Pipeline
Description of layout algorithm: Minimization of wiggle, crossings, and whitespace by genetic algorithm with postprocessing. Interactions can be multiple timesteps long. A genome assigns each interaction an integer $y$-slot. To evaluate a genome, a pipeline approach of rearranging lines inside slots and removing whitespace is applied and then a fitness function is employed that measures wiggle and crossings. To rearrange lines inside a slot, lines are categorized and greedily made as straight as possible. Minimizing whitespace greedily shifts slot segments (Connected clusters inside a slot) down. An adaptation incorporating locations is presented, where a fixed order of locations is also encoded with a genome.
Evaluation: Look at picture (paper says case study)
Venue: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics

Optimization Objectives: Compactness - limit whitespace, Crossings - pair crossings, Wiggles/Bends - quadratic wiggle height, Wiggles/Bends - wiggle count
Authors: S. Liu, and Y. Wu, and E. Wei, and M. Liu, and Y. Liu
Title: StoryFlow: Tracking the Evolution of Stories
Method Type: Exact - Linear Programming, Heuristic - Longest Common Subsequence wiggle count minimization, Heuristic - Sugiyama-like ordering, Heuristic - greedy, Pipeline
Description of layout algorithm:
Pipeline approach. First reducing crossings, then wiggle number, then wiggle distance, and whitespace. After locations are sorted greedily, crossings are optimized as in standard storylines but with the location restrictions. This is done in Sugiyama style, barycenter heuristic considering the hierarchy.
To minimize the wiggle count in the next step, the problem is heuristically reduced to the longest common subsequence problem.
Next, given the ordering constraints and constraints on some lines being straight, a quadratic program minimizes squared wiggle height and white space given two weights.
Evaluation: Expert interview, Look at picture (paper says case study), Quantitative metrics (crossings etc)
Constraints / Assumptions: Equidistant interaction characters, Interaction grouping, Minimum character separation, Single cluster/location ordering
Venue: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics

Authors: J. -K. Chou, and I. Liao, and K. -L. Ma, and C. -K. Yang
Title: A Study on Enhancing Timeline-Like Visualization with Verbal Text
Venue: 2013 International Conference on Cyberworlds

Authors: Erk Ediz Akyiğit; Tuğkan Cengiz; Onur Burak Yildirim; Selim Balcisoy
Title: Visual exploratory tool for storyline generation
Venue: 2014 IEEE Conference on Visual Analytics Science and Technology (VAST)

Optimization Objectives: Crossings - pair crossings, Wiggles/Bends - quadratic wiggle height
Authors: Makarand Tapaswi, Martin Bauml, Rainer Stiefelhagen
Title: StoryGraphs: Visualizing Character Interactions as a Timeline
Visual/Structural features supported: Labels - characters, Other - encoding non-interacting characters
Method Type: Heuristic - gradient descent, Heuristic - objective abstraction
Description of layout algorithm:
A ccombined objective function of proximity, squared wiggle height, and
crossings is given. It computes coordinates. The crossing ob-
jective is abstracted into a heuristic one (pseudo-Huber loss)
that is differentiable. Then matlab fmincon is used to compute
a solution with small combined objective function.

Proximity models that characters an interactions are together. Separation models a minimum separation between characters.

Alough there is a quantitative study, this is on the scene detection from video and not on the storyline.
Venue: Proceedings of the IEEE Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR) 2014

Optimization Objectives: Compactness - limit whitespace, Crossings - pair crossings, Wiggles/Bends - wiggle height
Authors: Qiang Lu; Xiang-yuan Zhu; Li Liu; Shu-bo Cao
Title: An effective demonstration for group collaboration based on storyline visualization technology
Method Type: Exact - Linear Programming, Heuristic - Sugiyama-like ordering, Pipeline
Description of layout algorithm:
Crossings are minimized by a sugiyama style sweep.
A greedy algorithm is used for one step, but the given refer-
ence for that algorithm is not in the paper. It is not properly
described how wiggle and whitespace is minimize, at least not understandable to me. 

Wiggle height and whitespace is minimized with linear programming as far as I understand, even though the paper says wiggle count is minimized.
Venue: Proceedings of the 2014 IEEE 18th International Conference on Computer Supported Cooperative Work in Design (CSCWD)

Optimization Objectives: Distance approximation, Wiggles/Bends - quadratic wiggle height
Authors: Pascal Held; Christian Braune; Rudolf Kruse
Title: Exploring Dinofun park happenings

Real-world time series with distance values. The
quadratic difference to the real world distance plus squared
wiggle height is minimized. A proper objective function is
given, but the method to minimize it is not described.
Evaluation: Look at picture (paper says case study)
Venue: 2015 IEEE Conference on Visual Analytics Science and Technology (VAST)

Authors: Shejuti Silvia, June Abbas, Sam Huskey, Chris Weaver
Title: Storyline Visualization with Force Directed Layout
Description of layout algorithm:
Using force-directed algorithm only for y-coordinate.
Nodes in an interaction attract each other. Nodes connected
by an edge attract each other (same entity over consecitive
timesteps). Nodes in an interaction repulse each other.
Venue: The Digital Latin Library Project, The University of Oklahoma

Optimization Objectives: Compactness - limit whitespace, Crossings - pair crossings, Wiggles/Bends - wiggle count
Authors: Dustin L. Arendt
Title: SVEN: An Alternative Storyline Framework for Dynamic Graph Visualization
Method Type: Exact - Linear Programming, Heuristic - maximum independent set wiggle count, Pipeline
Description of layout algorithm: Pipeline approach. Crossings are minimimized by modelling the storyline as a directed acyclic graph where each event corresponds to a node. Then graphvis is used to minimize crossings (probably sugiyama-style). Wiggle count is minimized heuristically by modelling the problem as maximum independent set. Linear programming is used for minimizing whitespace.
Venue: Graph Drawing and Network Visualization

Authors: Irina Kostitsyna, Martin Nollenburg, Valentin Polishchuk, Andre Schulz, and Darren Strash
Title: On Minimizing Crossings in Storyline Visualizations
Description of layout algorithm:
Minimizing crossings: It is shown that if meetings only
consist of two entities and the meetings form a tree, a solution
with O(n log n) crossings can be computed, which is also a tight lower bound. Further, an FPT-
algorithm in the number of characters is given.

Also NP-completeness by a simple reduction from bipartite crossing number is hinted.
Venue: Graph Drawing and Network Visualization

Authors: Y. Zheng, and W. Wu, and H. Qu, and C. Ma, and L. M. Ni
Title: Visual analysis of bi-directional movement behavior
Description of layout algorithm: No layout description, but heuristic is mentioned. Two different storyline views are presented, but both are pretty far from what a real storyline is used for.
Venue: 2015 IEEE International Conference on Big Data (Big Data)

Optimization Objectives: Compactness - limit whitespace, Crossings - pair crossings, Wiggles/Bends - wiggle count
Authors: Y. Tanahashi, and C. -H. Hsueh, and K. -L. Ma
Title: An Efficient Framework for Generating Storyline Visualizations from Streaming Data
Method Type: Exact - Linear Programming, Heuristic - Longest Common Subsequence wiggle count minimization, Heuristic - Sugiyama-like ordering, Heuristic - greedy, Heuristic - local search, Pipeline
Description of layout algorithm:
Similar as in [Tanahashi, 2012] Pipeline approach. slots are used to as-
sign interaction sessions, as in [Tanahashi, 2012]. First, a greedy heuristic is
used to assign these slots such that there are few wiggles and
crossings. Then, crossings are minimized by greedily assign-
ing order from last timestep to the next for shared characters.
Non-shared are appended at the end. Wiggle count is min-
imized with longest common subsequence. Wiggle height is
minimized with quadratic programming, adapting [Storyflow, 2013]. At the
end a local search is used, to move location sessions between
slots. The authors claim that the layout process is for streaming data (where new timesteps may be added at the end). I am not sure though if the layout algorithm is majorly different from one that is not dealing with streaming data.
Constraints / Assumptions: Equidistant interaction characters, Interaction grouping, Minimum character separation
Venue: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics

Authors: Shejuti Silvia, Ronak Etemadpour, June Abbas, Sam Huskey and Chris Weaver
Title: Visualizing Variation in Classical Text with Force Directed Storylines
Description of layout algorithm: Layout algorithm based on Fruchtermann-Reingold force-directed. Different forces pull interactions together, force lines to be straight, and separate different interactions. It seems that the exact y-coordinates are computed afterwards, but the description for that is missing.
Venue: Workshop on visualization for the digital humanities 2016

Authors: Martin Gronemann, Michael Junger, Frauke Liers, and Francesco Mambelli
Title: Crossing Minimization in Storyline Visualization
Description of layout algorithm:
Exact algorithm for crossings minimization using ILP building on
a reduciton to max-cut.
Venue: Graph Drawing and Network Visualization

Optimization Objectives: Compactness - limit whitespace, Crossings - pair crossings, Wiggles/Bends - wiggle count, Wiggles/Bends - wiggle height
Authors: L. Qiang, and C. Bingjie
Title: StoryCake: A Hierarchical Plot Visualization Method for Storytelling in Polar Coordinates
Method Type: Heuristic - Longest Common Subsequence wiggle count minimization, Heuristic - Sugiyama-like ordering, Pipeline
Description of layout algorithm:
Uses storyflow:Pipeline approach. First crossings are minimzed with
sweep + barycenter heuristic. Then wiggle count is minimizes
with longest common subsequence. How wiggle height and
whitespace is minimized is impossible to parse.
Evaluation: Look at picture (paper says case study), User study

Authors: K. Osmakčić; K. Kocijan
Title: Story of a 'Storyline Visualization' in High School Readings

Optimization Objectives: Crossings - pair crossings, Wiggles/Bends - wiggle count
Authors: Yuhua Liu, Hanfei Lin, Yitao Liang, Changbo Wang
Title: An application of optimization method for storyline based on cluster analysis
Method Type: Heuristic - Longest Common Subsequence wiggle count minimization, Heuristic - MDS, Heuristic - Sugiyama-like ordering, Pipeline
Description of layout algorithm: First characters are partitioned into cluster based on 1D multidimensional scaling. The characters then by default (if they are in no interaction), are within their cluster. Interactions are then assigned to the clusters based on how many characters of the interaction are in the cluster. Then the storyflow algorithm is used: barycenter-like crossing minimization. Longest common subsequence wiggle count minimization.
Constraints / Assumptions: Equidistant interaction characters, Interaction grouping, Minimum character separation
Venue: VINCI '17: Proceedings of the 10th International Symposium on Visual Information Communication and Interaction

Authors: Thomas C. van Dijk, Martin Fink, Norbert Fischer, Fabian Lipp, Peter Markfelder, Alexander Ravsky, Subhash Suri, and Alexander Wolff
Title: Block Crossings in Storyline Visualizations
Method Type: Approximation, Exact - FPT, Heuristic - greedy ordering, Theory
Description of layout algorithm:
Minimization of block crossings, which are cross-
ings between two bundles of edges. They show poly for 0
block crossings, FPT in number of characters. Greedy al-
gorithm for meetings of size 2 which yields optimal solution
for 3 entities. Constant factor approximation for constant-size
meetings, where meetings cannot be repeated. Further, they also show NP-hardness of block crossing minimization. They also improve the FPT-algorithm for pair crossings parameterized by the number of characters.
Evaluation: Benchmarks, Quantitative metrics (crossings etc), Theory paper
Source: Journal of Graph Algorithms and Applications
Venue: Journal of Graph Algorithms and Applications

Optimization Objectives: Crossings - pair crossings, Wiggles/Bends - wiggle count, Wiggles/Bends - wiggle height

Title: Minimizing Wiggles in Storyline Visualizations

Character curve coordinates must adhere to matrix en-
tries. Exact minimization of crossings, wiggle count, wiggle
height. Multi-objective formulation with ILP and SAT
Venue: Graph Drawing and Network Visualization

Thomas C. van Dijk, Fabian Lipp(B) , Peter Markfelder,

Authors: D. Arendt, and M. Pirrung
Title: The “y” of it Matters, Even for Storyline Visualization
Method Type: Exact - Linear Programming, Heuristic - Sugiyama-like ordering, Heuristic - local search, Heuristic - maximum independent set wiggle count, Pipeline
Description of layout algorithm:
The paper compares non-standard storyline (which does not fall within our definition) with standard storylines. The standard storyline algorithm: Crossings are mini-
mized by sweep + Median heuristic. Then a local search
swaps neighboring nodes. Wiggle count is minimized by
modelling as independent set (cannot use lcs because mul-
tiple in/out-edges) and heuristic for independent set is used.
Whitespace is reduced with linear programming.
For the case where entities are grouped into locations per
timesteps and locations have a fixed order: Single node per lo-
cation with edge-weighted directed graph. Greedy algorithm
is used.

Optimization Objectives: Compactness - limit whitespace, Crossings - pair crossings, Wiggles/Bends - quadratic wiggle height, Wiggles/Bends - wiggle count
Authors: T. Tang, and S. Rubab, and J. Lai, and W. Cui, and L. Yu, and Y. Wu
Title: iStoryline: Effective Convergence to Hand-drawn Storylines
Method Type: Exact - Linear Programming, Heuristic - Longest Common Subsequence wiggle count minimization, Heuristic - Sugiyama-like ordering, Pipeline
Description of layout algorithm:
Interactive tool to assist storyline drawing. So interactions impose constraints on the layout process. In general, the storyflow algorithm is used. 

When constraints on character order are imposed, a heuristic from [Forster, 2005 is used] which essentially is an adaptation of the barycenter heuristic. 

Constraints can also say that character coordinates must behave in a specific way, which is encoded in the alignment (wiggle count, wiggle height, whitespace reduction) step.

Further interactions also change the alignment step.
Constraints / Assumptions: Equidistant interaction characters, Interaction grouping, Minimum character separation, User constraints
Venue: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics

Optimization Objectives: Compactness - limit whitespace, Crossings - block crossings, Wiggles/Bends

Title: Storyline Visualizations with Ubiquitous Actors
Method Type: Exact - SAT, Heuristic - local search, Heuristic - other
Description of layout algorithm:
Entities are not a single path but a tree because they can
participate in multiple groups at a single point in time. The
trees are computed heuristically. Then the SAT approach of van Dijk et al. for
minimizing block crossings is applied. Then local search
is applied to further reduce crossings. Although wiggle and whitespace is also minimized, it is not described how.
Evaluation: Look at picture (paper says case study)

Method Type: Exact - Linear Programming, Heuristic - AI, Heuristic - Longest Common Subsequence wiggle count minimization, Heuristic - Sugiyama-like ordering, Pipeline
Description of layout algorithm:
This is an extension to istoryline, where reinforcement learn-
ing is used to compute storylines which adhere to constraints
that are obtained from interactions.
The initial layout is computed the same as istoryline. Then the user modifies the layout, and the AI agent can propose different variants of new layouts, and the user can choose one of them.
Also, a new set of interactions ins introduced.

The AI agent tries to predict what a user would do to change the layout from the initial layout and the user-adapted layout.

Authors: Alexander Dobler, Tim Hegemann, Martin Nöllenburg, Alexander Wolff
Title: Optimizing Wiggle in Storylines
Method Type: Exact - ILP, Exact - Linear Programming, Exact - dynamic programming, Theory
Domain: Movies/Books, Rolling stock schedules, Social - other
Description of layout algorithm: Theory+application paper for wiggle minimization. Polynomial (Q)LP for wiggle height and quadratic wiggle height. Wiggle count minimization is shown to be NP-hard, DP for two time steps. Also, drawing style with circular arcs: width between two time steps is minimized.
Evaluation: Benchmarks, Quantitative metrics (crossings etc), Theory paper
Constraints / Assumptions: Fixed character ordering in interaction, Interaction grouping, Minimum character separation
Venue: Graph Drawing and Network Visualization

Optimization Objectives: Crossings - pair crossings, Fairness, Skewness, Wiggles/Bends - wiggle height
Authors: Tommaso Piselli, Giuseppe Liotta, Fabrizio Montecchiani, Martin Nöllenburg, Sara Di Bartolomeo
Title:
F2Stories: A Modular Framework for Multi-Objective
Optimization of Storylines with a Focus on Fairness
Description of layout algorithm: MILP for computing the complete layout. Characters are placed at discrete coordinates with equal spacing. Standard ILP for crossing minimization. Wiggle and Skewness (number of characters to remove to be planar) are also optimized. All these criteria have adjustable parameters with which they can be weighted in the objective. Further, also fairness of wiggle, skewness, and crossings can be optimized, weighted with further parameters. A post-processing wiggle minimization is also proposed when the ILP does not optimize wiggle. In that case, characters do not have equal spacing. Iteratively and greedily groups of characters are moved up or down as long as wiggle is reduced
Evaluation: Benchmarks, Look at picture (paper says case study), Quantitative metrics (crossings etc)
Venue: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics

Authors: Mingdong Zhang, Li Chen, Junhai Yong
Title: Stratiline: A visualization system based on stratified storyline
Method Type: Heuristic - Global ordering, Heuristic - MDS, Heuristic - Sugiyama-like ordering
Description of layout algorithm: Focus on locations. First, actors, interactions, and locations, get a significance score (weight). The single location order is computed with MDS based on number of characters going between locations. Actors have a global order, and this order is used to sort actors in events. Then a barycenter-like post-processing is used. Y-coordinates do not need to be computed, as locations are rows and actors are equally spaced.
Evaluation: Look at picture (paper says case study), Quantitative metrics (crossings etc)
Venue: Computers & Graphics

Authors: Jie Lin, Chuan-Kai Yang, Chiun-How Kao
Title: Visualizing NBA information via storylines
Description of layout algorithm: The algorithm is not super clear to me. First, events are spaced out so that crowded regions are avoided. Then, the ordering is computed with TSP sorting or Ellipse sorting.
Venue: Computers & Graphics

Optimization Objectives: Compactness - limit whitespace, Crossings - pair crossings, Wiggles/Bends - wiggle count
Authors: Y. -H. Kuo, and D. Liu, and K. -L. Ma
Title: SpreadLine: Visualizing Egocentric Dynamic Influence
Method Type: Heuristic - Longest Common Subsequence wiggle count minimization, Heuristic - Sugiyama-like ordering, Heuristic - greedy white space reduction, Pipeline
Domain: Authorship network, Disease outbreak, Social - other
Description of layout algorithm:
Egocentric storyline of a ego network, ego is straight line. Distinction between 1-alters and 2-alters. Stronger ties are closer to the ego.  Those receiving to ego are below, those sending above. So the ordering is nearly determined by the Data. The layout generation adapts storyflow: The 2-alters that still can change their order are sorted by barycenter. Wiggle count is still minimized with lcs, but ego needs to stay straight.
The compaction step is different from storyflow: A greedy algorithm is used to compact and make lines more straight, the description is not reproducible.
Evaluation: Look at picture (paper says case study), User study
Venue: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
